claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch has boundary snapshot auditing, agent tool profile support, and project-mutation detection implemented in evidence-dispatch.ts.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() creates file digests for boundary audit
evidence.why: Core boundary audit implementation that fingerprints project files

evidence.summary: execution_policy boundary config: project_mutation=forbidden, allowed_write_roots=[artifact dir]
evidence.why: Defines the boundary control policy for evidence agents

evidence.summary: agentToolProfile() reads agent tool profile from options/input with default 'boundary'
evidence.why: Tool profile configuration point that determines agent permissions

evidence.summary: Claude provider normalizes tool profile to 'research', 'full', or 'boundary'; disables session persistence
evidence.why: Shows how agent_tool_profile maps to Claude Code settings

evidence.summary: Runtime builds boundary snapshots before/after agent execution; diffs changes to detect mutations
evidence.why: Actual boundary audit execution in runtime that catches violations

evidence.summary: Boundary violations add BLOCKING_BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers to readiness
evidence.why: Shows how boundary violations block PRD readiness

evidence.summary: sanitizeMissing() filters out non-missing status items from missing arrays
evidence.why: Prevents false positive blockers from status notes like 'no missing data'

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors() validates evidence records declare scope as project/external/user/unknown
evidence.why: Schema validation that enforces evidence scope requirements

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer/cross-checker/verifier protocols with writes_code=false
evidence.why: Protocol definitions that should forbid project mutations

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope() infers scope from record fields; maps external/web/user/project keywords
evidence.why: Scope inference logic for validating evidence records

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() blocks when project facts lack project-scoped evidence with paths
evidence.why: Readiness gate that requires project evidence with file locators

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness() checks for required evidence agent roles and scope issues
evidence.why: Main PRD readiness logic that validates evidence agent results
Tool restriction mechanism that could be used for boundary control

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt() includes boundary rules in agent prompt: 'do not modify target project files'
evidence.why: Agent instruction that states the boundary constraint in natural language

assumptions: Agent tool profile 'full' allows full Claude Code tools but boundary policy still forbids project mutations
Boundary snapshot audit uses SHA256 file digests to detect modifications
Evidence agents are spawned via child_process spawn with stdio pipes
risks: If boundary snapshot before/after comparison is buggy or has false negatives, project mutations could go undetected
If agent tool profile 'full' grants write tools, the prompt-level boundary instruction may not be enforced at runtime
If allowed_write_roots is not correctly resolved, artifacts might leak into project directories
missing: No evidence that Claude Code agent tool permissions actually respect prompt-level boundary instructions
No evidence that boundary audit has been stress-tested with real Claude -p full-tool agents
No evidence showing how provider-adapter tool restrictions interact with evidence-dispatch boundary policy

result.notes: Evidence confirms boundary snapshot auditing exists and is implemented in evidence-dispatch.ts. Missing runtime verification that prompt-level boundary constraints are actually enforced by Claude Code agents.
